pattern: Small-to-large merging of distinct path-XOR sets up the tree answers each subtree's order-statistic query without recomputing from scratch.
hints: First turn the tree into a value per node: the running XOR of values along the path from the root.
Each subtree's answer depends only on the set of distinct path-XOR values it contains, so build those sets bottom up.
Merge children sets into the parent by always folding the smaller set into the larger one; once a queried node's full distinct set is ready, sort it once and read off the k-th value or report -1.

edge_inputs.description: two children with duplicate path xor collapse to one distinct value
chain where each subtree adds distinct xors
single node, k of one then beyond
k exceeds the number of distinct values
leaf subtree has exactly one path xor
zero values keep prefix xor unchanged
branching tree mixing distinct and repeated xors

stress.description: random moderate tree (parent always a smaller index) with many queries over a narrow value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    par = [-1] * n
    for i in range(1, n):
        par[i] = rng.randrange(0, i)
    vals = [rng.randrange(0, 40) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, n // 2)
    queries = [[rng.randrange(0, n), rng.randrange(1, n + 1)] for _ in range(q)]
    return [par, vals, queries]
stress.note: narrow values force duplicate path xors so distinct sets shrink

stress.description: bushier random tree with wider values and full set of queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    par = [-1] * n
    for i in range(1, n):
        par[i] = rng.randrange(max(0, i - 5), i)
    vals = [rng.randrange(0, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    queries = [[rng.randrange(0, n), rng.randrange(1, n + 1)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [par, vals, queries]
stress.note: shallow bushy tree with mostly-distinct path xors
